Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Clayton P. Roche 
Deputy Attorney General 
Department of Justice 
July 7, 1988 
350 McAllister street, Room 6000 
San Francisco, California 
94102 
" ( 
Dear Mr. Roche: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-88-221 
You have written requesting the Commission's views 
regarding an opinion request pending with your office. 
To the 
extent that the issues raised in your letter relate to the 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").!! they 
appear to ~rise from the provisions of Government Code Section 
89001. 
This section was recently amended by the voters on 
June 7 when they adopted Proposition 73. 
The Commission has issued two ihterim advice letters on 
this subject. 
Copies are enclosed for your use. 
The 
Commission will address this issue further at its July 26, 
1988, meeting. 
We will keep you apprised of the outcome·. 
REL: Id 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths

~8 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660 


JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP 
Attorney General 
June 
3, 1988 
John H. Larson, Chairman 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, Calif 
9~8~6 
Dear Mr. Larson: 
Opinion No. 88-502 
State of California 
DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE 
350 McALLISTER STREET, ROOM 6000 
SAN FRANCISCO 94102 
(415) 557-2544 
(415) 5 
-
586 
Our opinion has been requested on the following questions: 
1. 
Maya legislator send unsolicited information about 
voter registration to his or her constituents? 
2. 
Maya legislator send unsolicited voter registration 
affidavits to his or her constituents? 
3. 
Maya legislator have voter registration affidavits 
available in either the Capitol office or a district office for 
members of the public? 
4. 
May the Assembly authorize its employees to become 
deputy registrars and to register qualified citizens in Assembly 
offices during regular working hours? 
You are invited to submit whatever views you may have on the 
question presented. 
To be of the most aid to me in preparing a 
proposed response, views should reach me by July 1, 1988. 
However, views received after that date and until final approval 
of the opinion by the Attorney General will still be considered. 
Very truly yours, 
JOHN K. VAN DE KAMP 
CPR: is 
nc • 


• 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Clayton P. Roche 
Deputy Attorney General 
Department of Justice 
June 16, 1988 
350 McAllister street, Room 6000 
San Francisco, CA 
94102 
Re: 
88-221 
Dear Mr. Roche: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on June 15, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice re~est, you may contact Robert Leidigh, an attorney in 
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 
18329).} 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
Very truly yours, 
0~\~ 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
